description: Good-enough mapping between cp_measure feature names and CellProfiler 4.x feature names. This is NOT exhaustive and NOT guaranteed to be perfect. Use it to flag clear incompatibilities; do not assume everything maps cleanly.
notes: CellProfiler embeds the image/channel name and object name inside the feature string. cp_measure does not — channel is handled by the caller (e.g. via featurizer.py's __ separator).
Texture: the image name is inserted BETWEEN the Haralick feature name and the scale, e.g. cp_measure 'AngularSecondMoment_3_00_256' → CellProfiler 'Texture_AngularSecondMoment_{image}_3_00_256'.
RadialDistribution: image name inserted between metric and bin, e.g. 'RadialDistribution_FracAtD_1of4' → 'RadialDistribution_FracAtD_{image}_1of4'.
Some cp_measure features (moments, InertiaTensor, etc.) have no direct CellProfiler equivalent.
Some CellProfiler features (e.g. AreaShape_HullSolidity) may not exist in cp_measure.
Confidence levels: high = math and name verified; medium = name matches, math assumed equivalent; low = plausible match, unverified.

sizeshape.naming_rule: AreaShape_{cp_name}  (no channel needed — shape is image-independent)

BoundingBoxArea.note: No direct CellProfiler equivalent; closest is bounding box min/max

SpatialMoment_0_0.note: cp_measure only — CellProfiler does not expose raw spatial moments

texture.note: The image name is inserted INSIDE the feature string in CellProfiler, not appended. This makes automated translation fragile.

radial_distribution.naming_rule: RadialDistribution_{metric}_{image}_{bin}  (image inserted between metric and bin)
radial_distribution.note: Like texture, the image name is inserted inside the string, not appended.

radial_zernikes.naming_rule: RadialDistribution_{ZernikeType}_{n}_{m}_{image}  — WARNING: CellProfiler appends image; cp_measure does not include it
radial_zernikes.note: Zernike phase and magnitude naming may differ between versions. Treat as low-confidence until validated against benchmark data.

cp_measure_only: All moment features (SpatialMoment_*, CentralMoment_*, NormalizedMoment_*, HuMoment_*, InertiaTensor*)
BoundingBoxArea (as a single feature; CellProfiler exposes bounding box via min/max coordinates)
cellprofiler_only: Location_X, Location_Y (centroid from Identify module, not from measure)
Any features from modules not implemented in cp_measure (e.g. MeasureObjectNeighbors details)

rotation_sensitivity_study.description: RSI (Rotation Sensitivity Index) scores from a 115,200-row simulation: 1,600 synthetic cells × 72 angles (0–355° in 5° steps). RSI = within-cell variance across angles / between-cell variance at angle=0. See github.com/gwaybio/cellgenerator analysis/ for full notebooks and methodology.

rsi_categories.stable: RSI < 0.01  — rotation contributes < 1% of biological variance
rsi_categories.test_artefact: Appears sensitive due to symmetric test shapes; genuinely invariant
rsi_categories.constant: Zero between-cell variance in this dataset; RSI undefined

corrections.moment_rotation_correction: Binomial rotation transform applied to all (p,q) moments simultaneously
corrections.phase_minus_m_times_orientation: phase_corrected = phase + m × angle_rad  (sign confirmed empirically)
corrections.convert_to_distance_from_centroid: sqrt((X-cx)²+(Y-cy)²); replace X/Y pair with scalar distance
corrections.pre_orient_only: No analytical post-hoc fix; rotate image to canonical orientation before measurement
